internal_forces.py¶

Internal forces utility class for uniform distributed load on standard structural systems.

Author: Elliot Melcer

class InternalForces[Quellcode]¶

Bases: object

Utility class (not instantiable) for computing internal forces on standard structural systems under uniformly distributed load.

Position convention: x is normalized, with x = 0 at the first (outer) support and x = 1 at the next support (x = 2 at the one after, etc.).

static validate_x_position(system: SystemType, x_position: float) → None[Quellcode]¶

Validate that an x-position is within the valid range for a given system.

Parameter:

  • system (SystemType) – Structural system type.

  • x_position (float) – Normalized position to validate [-].

Verursacht:

ValueError – If system is not in MAX_X_POSITIONS, or if x_position is outside [0, max_x] for that system.

static get_moment_data(system: SystemType, moment_type: MomentType) → Dict[str, float][Quellcode]¶

Return the moment coefficient and governing x-position from the lookup table.

Parameter:

  • system (SystemType) – Structural system type.

  • moment_type (MomentType) – Governing moment type (MAX_POS_MOMENT or MAX_NEG_MOMENT).

Rückgabe:

Dictionary with keys:

  • "coefficient" — moment coefficient α such that M = α · w · L² [-].

  • "x_position" — normalized position where the governing moment occurs [-].

Rückgabetyp:

dict

Verursacht:

ValueError – If system or moment_type is not found in MOMENT_DATA.

static calculate_moment_kNm(slab_construction: SlabConstruction, loads: Loads, system: SystemType = SystemType.SIMPLE_BEAM, combination: str = 'FUNDAMENTAL', x_norm: float | None = None, moment: MomentType | None = None) → float[Quellcode]¶

Calculate the bending moment at a specific position or the governing maximum moment for a system under uniformly distributed load.

Exactly one of x_norm or moment must be provided.

Parameter:

  • slab_construction (SlabConstruction) – Full slab construction object (provides span and dead loads).

  • loads (Loads) – Loads object (provides live loads and combination factors).

  • system (SystemType, optional) – Structural system type. Default is SystemType.SIMPLE_BEAM.

  • combination (str, optional) – Load combination name. Default is "FUNDAMENTAL".

  • x_norm (float, optional) – Normalized position along the beam (0 at first support, 1 at second support, etc.) [-]. Use this to compute M(x) at an arbitrary position via the analytical moment function. Requires the system to have an entry in MOMENT_FUNCTIONS.

  • moment (MomentType, optional) – Governing moment type. Use this to look up the maximum moment via the coefficient table. Mutually exclusive with x_norm.

Rückgabe:

Bending moment [kNm]. Sign follows the moment function convention (positive = sagging for x_norm; sign from coefficient table for moment).

Rückgabetyp:

float

Verursacht:

  • ValueError – If both or neither of x_norm and moment are provided, or if the moment coefficient is 0.0 (moment type not applicable for this system).

  • NotImplementedError – If x_norm is provided but no moment function is implemented for system.

Examples

Moment at a specific position (requires implemented moment function):

>>> M = InternalForces.calculate_moment_kNm(
...     slab, loads, system=SystemType.SIMPLE_BEAM, x_norm=0.3
... )

Maximum positive moment via coefficient table:

>>> M_max = InternalForces.calculate_moment_kNm(
...     slab, loads, system=SystemType.THREE_SPAN,
...     moment=MomentType.MAX_POS_MOMENT
... )
static moment_simple_beam(x: float, w: float, L: float) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the bending moment at position x for a simply supported beam.

Parameter:

  • x (float) – Position along the beam [m].

  • w (float) – Uniformly distributed load [kN/m].

  • L (float) – Span length [m].

Rückgabe:

Bending moment M(x) [kNm] (positive = sagging).

Rückgabetyp:

float

static moment_two_span(x: float, w: float, L: float)[Quellcode]¶

Compute the bending moment at position x for a two-span continuous beam with equal spans under uniform load.

Parameter:

  • x (float) – Position along the beam [m], measured from the first outer support. Valid range: 0 ≤ x ≤ 2L.

  • w (float) – Uniformly distributed load [kN/m].

  • L (float) – Length of one span [m].

Rückgabe:

Bending moment M(x) [kNm]. Returns 0 outside the valid range.

Rückgabetyp:

float

static moment_cantilever(x: float, w: float, L: float) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the bending moment at position x for a cantilever beam.

Parameter:

  • x (float) – Position along the beam [m] (0 at fixed support, L at free end).

  • w (float) – Uniformly distributed load [kN/m].

  • L (float) – Span length [m].

Rückgabe:

Bending moment M(x) [kNm] (negative = hogging).

Rückgabetyp:

float
